text:
There once was a wise old bookworm who lived inside a towering elm tree. Every autumn, he'd watch as leaves changed colors and fell. Once, feeling lonely, he decided to read a book aloud. He began with "A Midsummer Night's Dream" by Shakespeare.

As he spoke, fairies danced around him. They giggled and whispered secrets, transforming him into various creatures: an owl wise beyond belief, a playful deer, even a tiny pixie. Through his tale, the forest came alive, creatures enjoying their enchanted evening.

Eventually, the moon rose giant, casting gleaming lights over sleepy sleeping woodlands. The bookworm transformed back into himself just as dawn stole first rays of daylight.

Each nighttime reading became a special time, weaving stories that brought friends and foes together. The bookworm felt happier than ever, knowing that through words, he could create worlds.

So each year, as new leaves unfurled, he'd read aloud again, weaving dreams through whispers of
